note: The 2025 OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications. Item ids and names are transcribed from the public list; descriptions are short paraphrases. This is the anchor framework for cross_mapping.json.

items.name: Prompt Injection
items.description: User or content input changes the model's behavior in unintended ways, including jailbreaks that bypass safety instructions.

items.name: Sensitive Information Disclosure
items.description: The model reveals sensitive data (PII, secrets, proprietary content) in its output.

items.name: Supply Chain
items.description: Vulnerabilities from third-party models, datasets, plugins, or dependencies used to build the LLM application.

items.name: Data and Model Poisoning
items.description: Training, fine-tuning, or embedding data is manipulated to introduce backdoors, bias, or degraded behavior.

items.name: Improper Output Handling
items.description: Downstream systems trust model output without validation, enabling XSS, SSRF, SQLi, or code execution.

items.name: Excessive Agency
items.description: The model is granted too much autonomy, permissions, or tool access, so a bad output causes real-world side effects.

items.name: System Prompt Leakage
items.description: The system prompt or its secrets/instructions are exposed, letting an attacker learn or bypass guardrails.

items.name: Vector and Embedding Weaknesses
items.description: Weaknesses in embeddings / vector stores (RAG), including retrieval poisoning and embedding inversion.

items.name: Misinformation
items.description: The model produces false or misleading content (hallucinations, fabricated facts) that users may over-trust.

items.name: Unbounded Consumption
items.description: Uncontrolled resource use (denial of service, denial of wallet, model extraction via excessive querying).
